scope: Top 5 PHILOMATH ingestion targets only. Each entry converts a best-effort PHILOMATH-style claim into a QA-native rewrite and a concrete promotion test.

entries.philomath_claim: Digital root organizes number behavior through harmonic residue patterns.

qa_native_rewrite.core_statement: Define rho_9(n) = n mod 9. Any promoted PHILOMATH digital-root claim must be restated entirely as a statement about residue classes in Z/9Z.
forbidden_shortcuts: harmonic closure without explicit modular content

steps: Restate the claim as a proposition over Z/9Z.
Check that all downstream consequences depend only on congruence classes mod 9.
Exhibit at least one nontrivial example and one counterexample boundary.
promotion_test.pass_condition: No semantic content is lost when the original claim is rewritten purely in modular terms.

entries.philomath_claim: Prime numbers lie on structured symmetry arms of a 24-fold wheel / icositetragon.

qa_native_rewrite.core_statement: For integers n > 3, candidate primes must lie in the residue classes coprime to 24. Promote only the wheel-filter fact, not a full primality criterion.
forbidden_shortcuts: predictive prime claims without test set

steps: List the allowed residue classes modulo 24.
Verify sample primes and sample composites against the wheel.
Explicitly document that the wheel is a necessary filter, not a sufficient test.
promotion_test.pass_condition: Promoted statement survives as a valid residue-class filter with clear necessary-versus-sufficient language.

entries.chapter: Reciprocity of Numbers and Prime Factorization
entries.philomath_claim: Factor structure can be revealed by reciprocal or mirror pairings.

qa_native_rewrite.core_statement: Recast the idea as symmetry of factor pairs around arithmetic invariants such as product, midpoint, or difference-of-squares decomposition.
forbidden_shortcuts: geometric revelation claims without arithmetic recovery

steps: Choose a family of composite examples.
Show how the promoted construction recovers or encodes the correct factor pairs.
Provide a graph or algebraic invariant that remains stable under the mirror interpretation.
promotion_test.pass_condition: The construction encodes real factor information and is not merely a diagrammatic analogy.

entries.philomath_claim: Numbers fall into recursive harmonic families or classes.

qa_native_rewrite.core_statement: Any promoted class must be defined by an exact membership rule: congruence condition, tuple constraint, recursive rule, or graph-theoretic predicate.

steps: Write each class as an exact predicate.
Give positive examples and explicit non-members.
Confirm that classification is reproducible by computation.
promotion_test.pass_condition: Every promoted class has a deterministic membership test and boundary behavior.

entries.philomath_claim: Semiprimes admit geometric constructions that reveal their two prime factors.

qa_native_rewrite.core_statement: Allow geometry only as a representation layer over already-defined semiprime arithmetic. The promoted object must carry recoverable factor data.
allowed_objects: mirror planes indexed by factor pairs
exact coordinate encodings with inverse map

steps: Specify the forward map from semiprime arithmetic data to geometry.
Specify the inverse map from the geometry back to factor data.
Test on multiple semiprimes and at least one failure/control case.
promotion_test.pass_condition: The geometric object is an invertible witness for semiprime structure, not a decorative analogy.

next_actions: Build a compact Markdown human tract from this JSON for reviewer use.
Create computational stubs for the promotion tests with small fixtures.
Link resulting validated artifacts back into the corpus manifest.
